experienceData.description: Technical Support, serving public agencies in the municipality regarding information technology.

Technical leadership of the micro-frontends ecosystem with Module Federation. Architecture and development of DFL Learn super-app, Reviews App, Payments App and CI Reviewer Bot with AI. Mentoring fellows, defining technical standards, code reviews and participation in award-winning hackathons.

Programming and development teacher at Instituto Educar+, a vocational technology school. I teach programming logic, web development and best practices to beginner students, mentoring hands-on projects — students have presented projects at hackathons.

Leading architecture and development of internal projects. Defining technical standards, stack and best practices for the development team. Full-stack development of new systems with TypeScript, Next.js and NestJS. Integration with fiscal systems, Firebird and internal process automation.

about.title: About Me

about.bio1: Full-Stack Developer with 2 years of experience, specialized in micro-frontend architectures with Module Federation, AI integrations (Claude API, OpenAI) and process automation with n8n. Currently in the 3rd year of ADS and leading internal project architecture at M&M Informática.
about.bio2: At DevFellowship, I built the complete Module Federation ecosystem: DFL Learn (super-app host), Reviews App, Payments App and CI Reviewer Bot with AI. Participated in award-winning hackathons: ETH Latam 2025 (4th place - GreenLoop) and DevConnect ETH 2025 (2nd place Scroll track - TalentDAO).
about.bio3: Solid experience in React, TypeScript, Supabase, PostgreSQL, NestJS, Docker and CI/CD pipelines. Passionate about solving complex architecture problems and building intelligent automations that eliminate repetitive work.

deck.description: A personal command center for working with AI agents directly on my VPS. It brings together agent chat, live terminals, persistent memory, skills, token costs and host administration in a single web interface. Built entirely by myself, from scratch.
deck.longDescription: Deck is the platform I built — alone, from scratch — to be my command center when working with AI agents running on my own VPS. Instead of being stuck in an SSH terminal, Deck connects the browser straight to the machine: each session opens a Claude agent with isolated context, and I follow the execution in real time, with live tmux terminals and a persistent task queue. The app is split into areas covering the whole operation: Sessions (agent chat, model and active-skill selection per message), Contacts & Memory (the agent's persistent memory in navigable cards — facts, feedback and project context that survive across conversations), Documents (a markdown viewer for the repository's guides and learnings), Costs & Tokens (spend dashboard per session: input/output tokens, estimated cost and samples), Admin & Host (VPS health and inventory: CPU, RAM, load, disk, uptime, CLIs on PATH, connected MCP servers, tmux sessions and plugins, with sensitive actions protected by confirmation and an admin role on the relay) and in-app Documentation. Architecture: React + TypeScript + Vite frontend deployed on Vercel, a real-time Node backend (WebSocket) running on the Hetzner VPS, session orchestration via tmux, integration with the Claude API (Anthropic) and MCP servers, persistence in Supabase and secret management with Infisical. Everything designed, architected and implemented by me.

skillEvals.description: Developer challenges platform, where devs must challenge themselves regarding courses linked to the DevfellowshipAcademy course platform.
skillEvals.longDescription: Developer challenges platform, where devs must challenge themselves regarding courses linked to the DevfellowshipAcademy course platform. With screens for Mentorship, Administrators, everything well organized. Uses Judge0 as compilation API. Integrated as a mini app using Module Federation for modular integration with the main platform.

devfellowship.description: Main platform of the DevFellowship community, where developers connect, share knowledge and participate in collaborative projects.
devfellowship.longDescription: Main platform of the DevFellowship community, where developers connect, share knowledge and participate in collaborative projects. Modern interface with forums, events and integrated mentorship system.

codeLibrary.description: A landing page for a course platform called CodeLibrary that will be Devfellowship's next launch
codeLibrary.longDescription: A landing page for a course platform called CodeLibrary that will be Devfellowship's next launch. Project focused on developing the homepage of the community's new course platform.

greenloop.description: Web3 platform that transforms recyclable material deliveries into on-chain tokens. 4th place at ETH Latam 2025.
greenloop.longDescription: GreenLoop is a platform created during ETH Latam 2025 that records recyclables delivered to collection points and converts these deliveries into on-chain tokens, using Base network technology. We achieved 4th place in the hackathon.

talentdao.description: Web3 marketplace where companies create jobs, pay in USDC and generate NFTs as completed work credentials.
talentdao.longDescription: TalentDAO (DevConnect/MintWork) is a Web3 marketplace that connects companies and talents. Jobs are stored in smart contracts, with USDC escrow. When approved, it issues ERC-721 NFTs as permanent credentials. We achieved 2nd place in the Scroll track at DevConnect ETH 2025.

reviewRequests.description: DevFellowship's internal task review system, similar to Pull Requests.
reviewRequests.longDescription: Review Requests is an internal system developed for DevFellowship that allows creating, reviewing and evaluating tasks in a structured way. With complete review cycles, approval/rejection, reassessment and detailed history. Integrated as a mini app using Module Federation for modular integration with the main platform.

dflLearn.description: DevFellowship's super-app and Module Federation ecosystem host. Centralizes projects, Kanban, payments, meetings, LMS courses, CMDB and AI Chat in a single platform.
dflLearn.longDescription: DFL Learn is DevFellowship's complete operational platform — Module Federation host. Dashboard with Kanban board for tasks by status (To Do, In Progress, Dev Completed, Done), filters by project/epic/delivery and real-time metrics. Sidebar with modules: Projects, Tasks, Skills, Epics, Deliveries, Meetings, Reviews, Payments, Diagrams, Members and Plans. Multiple Supabase schemas and HookChat (AI via Claude API).

paymentsApp.description: Payments micro-frontend via Module Federation. Fellows generate invoices by selecting completed deliveries — the system calculates points × rate automatically.
paymentsApp.longDescription: DFL Payments manages fellow payments per delivery. The flow: select reference month + organization, the system lists available deliveries (all tasks in `dev_completed`), each with point value and rate (e.g., R$ 75.00/pt) calculating the total. One delivery per invoice. Tracks open invoices and history. Independent micro-frontend integrated via Module Federation into DFL Learn.

ciRevisorBot.description: CI bot running on GitHub Actions. Executes build, tests, lint and architecture checks — then posts inline comments and summary on the PR via Claude API.
ciRevisorBot.longDescription: DFL-Bot Reviewer is the shared CI system across all DevFellowship repos. Each PR triggers: build, Vitest, verify docs, lint, architecture checks, typecheck. Then the bot runs two steps: Inline review (analyzes the diff via Claude API and posts comments on changed lines) and Summary comment (consolidates the result on the PR). Example: 'Review submitted: 3 new inline comment(s), 0 file-level item(s)'. Based on DFL CI core, a reusable composite action.

hackathons.title: Hackathons & Events
hackathons.subtitle: Participation in blockchain and Web3 hackathons, with award-winning projects and enriching experiences

altpay.description: A PIX↔crypto payments solution where a credit score is decided on-chain. Chainlink CRE runs the risk logic on the DON and writes the result to contracts via CCIP, while Data Feeds provide a trusted price. Fund release is verifiable on Solana (Anchor/Rust) with a PIX payment proof. I worked on the frontend, backend and the full Chainlink and on-chain integration.

greenloop.description: Web3 platform that transforms deliveries of recyclable materials into on-chain tokens, encouraging sustainability through economic incentives. I participated in the frontend, backend and integration with smart contracts on the Base network.

talentdao.description: Web3 marketplace where companies can create jobs, pay in WETH and generate NFTs as credentials for completed work. I participated in contract integration, state flow and frontend integration with blockchain. We achieved 2nd place in the Scroll track at DevConnect.
